title: Comprehensive Workflow for Extracting High-Quality Bacterial DNA from Milk Samples
author: Panagiota STATHOPOULOU
materials: Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Adjust the final volume to 100 mL with nuclease-free water
steps.instruction: Adjust the final volume to 100 mL with nuclease-free water.

steps.title: Warm to 65 °C before use...
steps.instruction: Warm to 65 °C before use to ensure the CTAB is fully dissolved.

steps.title: To break down the protein matrix...
steps.instruction: To break down the protein matrix and release trapped bacteria, resuspend the washed pellet in 1 mL of 50-100 millimolar (mM) EDTA solution ( pH 8 ).

steps.title: After the 65°C incubation, centrifuge the lysate at > 13
steps.instruction: After the 65°C incubation, centrifuge the lysate at > 13.000 x g, °C, 5 minutes at room temperature. This will pellet any insoluble debris that could interfere with the magnetic beads. The clear liquid supernatant contains your DNA.

steps.title: Load Sample into MagCore® Cartridge:
steps.instruction: Load Sample into MagCore® Cartridge:

steps.title: Add Magnetic Beads:
steps.instruction: Add Magnetic Beads:

steps.title: Run Automated Protocol:
steps.instruction: Run Automated Protocol:

steps.title: Collect Purified DNA:
steps.instruction: Collect Purified DNA:

steps.title: If you chose Option A
steps.instruction: If you chose Option A: Resuspend the pellet in 5-10 mL of sterile PBS.

steps.title: Pellet Washing & Detergent Wash:
steps.instruction: Pellet Washing & Detergent Wash:

steps.title: Separating Milk Fractions
steps.instruction: Separating Milk Fractions: After centrifugation, three layers will be visible: a solid fat layer on top, the liquid skim milk, and a cell pellet at the bottom. Choose one of the following two paths:

steps.title: Option A (Standard Protocol - Pellet Only)
steps.instruction: Option A (Standard Protocol - Pellet Only): For routine analysis, carefully remove and discard the top fat layer and the middle liquid supernatant, leaving only the cell pellet. Proceed to Step 4.

steps.title: Option B (Comprehensive Protocol - Pellet + Fat Fraction)
steps.instruction: Option B (Comprehensive Protocol - Pellet + Fat Fraction): For the most accurate representation of the entire milk microbiome, it is important to process the fat fraction, as some bacterial species preferentially associate with it. Use a sterile spatula to carefully transfer the top fat layer into a new, clean 50 mL centrifuge tube. Aspirate and discard the middle liquid supernatant from the original tube, leaving the cell pellet. Resuspend the cell pellet in 1-2 mL of sterile PBS and transfer this suspension into the tube containing the fat fraction. This combines the two key fractions for co-extraction. Proceed to Step 4.

steps.title: If you chose Option B
steps.instruction: If you chose Option B: Resuspend the combined fat and pellet mixture in 5-10 mL of sterile PBS containing 0.1% Tween-20 to help dissolve residual fat and release associated bacteria. Vortex thoroughly. Centrifuge at 10.000 x g, °C, 10 minutes . Discard the supernatant. Perform one additional wash on the pellet using plain PBS to remove residual detergent. Centrifuge and discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Gram-Positive Lysis (Lysozyme)
steps.instruction: Gram-Positive Lysis (Lysozyme) : This step is essential for milk samples, which are likely to contain Gram-positive bacteria with tough cell walls.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 00:10:00 . The milky suspension should become clearer as casein micelles dissolve.

steps.title: Centrifuge at > 12
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at > 12.000 x g, °C, 5 minutes to pellet the cells. Carefully discard the supernatant.

steps.title: Prepare Lysozyme Solution
steps.instruction: Prepare Lysozyme Solution: Freshly prepare a 20 mg/mL Lysozyme solution by dissolving Lysozyme powder in the Lysozyme Reaction Buffer provided with the kit.

steps.title: Resuspend Pellet
steps.instruction: Resuspend Pellet: Resuspend the cell pellet in 200 µL of the freshly prepared Lysozyme solution. Incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 , mixing occasionally.

steps.title: After the lysozyme incubation, add 4...
steps.instruction: After the lysozyme incubation, add 4 µL of RNase A to the sample mixture.

steps.title: Vortex briefly to mix
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly to mix.

steps.title: Incubate at room temperature for 1 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 1 00:10:00 .

steps.title: To the lysozyme-treated sample, add 500...
steps.instruction: To the lysozyme-treated sample, add 500 µL of pre-warmed ( 65 °C ) CTAB Lysis Buffer .

steps.title: Add 20 µL of reconstituted Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of reconstituted Proteinase K from the RBC MagCore‱ kit.

steps.title: Vortex briefly and incubate at 65°C...
steps.instruction: Vortex briefly and incubate at 65°C for 1-2 hours , inverting the tube every 20-30 minutes to mix. The solution will become viscous as cells lyse.

steps.title: Using a clean pipette tip, transfer...
steps.instruction: Using a clean pipette tip, transfer the magnetic beads from the 'Beads Mixture' well (Well 9) of the cartridge into the same well containing your lysate (Well 11).

steps.title: This is the critical 'hand-off' step
steps.instruction: This is the critical 'hand-off' step. You will bypass the lysis step of the automated protocol. Carefully pipette the clear supernatant from Step 9 into the Lysis Buffer well (Well 11) of the RBC MagCore® pre-filled cartridge. Do not transfer any of the pelleted debris.

steps.title: Place the prepared cartridge into the MagCore® instrument
steps.instruction: Place the prepared cartridge into the MagCore® instrument. Select and run the standard pre-programmed Bacterial DNA extraction protocol (502). The instrument will now automatically perform the DNA binding, washing, and elution steps on your pre-cleaned lysate.

steps.title: Once the run is complete, retrieve...
steps.instruction: Once the run is complete, retrieve the elution tube containing your purified bacterial DNA. Store the DNA at -20 °C or below for long-term stability.

steps.title: To prepare 100 mL , dissolve...
steps.instruction: To prepare 100 mL , dissolve the following in nuclease-free water: 2 g CTAB (Cetyltrimethylammonium bromide), 8.18 g NaCl (for 1.4 Molarity (M) final concentration), 10 mL of 1 M Tris-HCl (for 100 mM final concentration, pH 8 ), 4 mL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA (for 20 millimolar (mM) final concentration, pH 8 ).

steps.title: Pour 10-40 mL of milk into...
steps.instruction: Pour 10-40 mL of milk into a 50 mL centrifuge tube. A larger starting volume is recommended for low-biomass samples.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10.000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes to pellet bacterial and somatic cells.

steps.title: SAFETY NOTE
steps.instruction: SAFETY NOTE: Phenol and chloroform are highly toxic and corrosive. Perform this step in a fume hood with appropriate personal protective equipment (gloves, lab coat, eye protection).

steps.title: Cool the lysate to room temperature
steps.instruction: Cool the lysate to room temperature.

steps.title: Add an equal volume (approx
steps.instruction: Add an equal volume (approx. 700 µL ) of phenol:chloroform:isoamyl alcohol (25:24:1).

steps.title: Vortex for 10 seconds to create...
steps.instruction: Vortex for 10 seconds to create an emulsion, then centrifuge at 14.000 x g, °C, 10 minutes to separate the phases.

steps.title: Carefully transfer the upper, clear aqueous...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the upper, clear aqueous phase (which contains the DNA) to a new sterile microcentrifuge tube. Do not disturb the white protein layer at the interface.

steps.title: Repeat the phenol:chloroform extraction on the...
steps.instruction: Repeat the phenol:chloroform extraction on the aqueous phase until the interface is clean and white, indicating that most proteins have been removed.

steps.title: To the final aqueous phase, add 0
steps.instruction: To the final aqueous phase, add 0.7 volumes of ice-cold isopropanol (e.g., if you have 500 µL of aqueous phase, add 350 µL of isopropanol).

steps.title: Mix gently by inverting the tube several times
steps.instruction: Mix gently by inverting the tube several times. You should see white, stringy DNA precipitate out of the solution.

steps.title: Incubate at -20 °C for at...
steps.instruction: Incubate at -20 °C for at least 00:30:00 to maximize DNA precipitation. Optionally incubate overnight.

steps.title: Pellet DNA:
steps.instruction: Pellet DNA:

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14.000 x g, 4 °C, 15 minutes . A small white pellet of DNA should be visible at the bottom of the tube.

steps.title: Wash DNA Pellet:
steps.instruction: Wash DNA Pellet:

steps.title: Add 500 µL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to the tube
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of ice-cold 70% ethanol to the tube. This step washes away residual salts and CTAB.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14.000 x g, 4 °C, 6 minutes

steps.title: Carefully decant the ethanol
steps.instruction: Carefully decant the ethanol. Repeat the 70% ethanol wash one more time for higher purity.

steps.title: Dry and Resuspend DNA:
steps.instruction: Dry and Resuspend DNA:

steps.title: After the final wash, remove as...
steps.instruction: After the final wash, remove as much ethanol as possible with a pipette.

steps.title: Air-dry the pellet for 5–10 minutes at room temperature
steps.instruction: Air-dry the pellet for 5–10 minutes at room temperature. Do not over-dry the pellet, as this can make it difficult to dissolve.

steps.title: Resuspend the DNA pellet in 30–50...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the DNA pellet in 30–50 µL of TE buffer or nuclease-free water. You may need to warm the tube to 65°C for 10 minutes to help dissolve the DNA completely. Store the purified DNA at -20°C.
